questions.question: Which state has the highest number of recognized startups as of March 2026 under the Startup India initiative?
questions.explanation: Under the Startup India initiative, more than 55,200 startups were recognized in FY 2025–26, the highest ever in a single year, taking the total to more than 2.23 lakh startups and generating more than 23.36 lakh jobs. Startup growth increased by 51.6% year-on-year, while job creation rose by 36.1%, showing strong expansion of India’s startup ecosystem. Around 48% of startups (more than 1.07 lakh) have at least one woman director or partner, indicating rising gender inclusion. Startups are now present across all States and Union Territories, with Maharashtra having the highest number of recognized startups (more than 38,660), followed by Karnataka, Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, and Gujarat.

questions.question: Who has been elected as the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha for the third consecutive term?
questions.explanation: Harivansh Narayan Singh has been elected Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha for the third consecutive term, and was elected unopposed. He is the first nominated member to hold this position, having been nominated by Droupadi Murmu after the retirement of Ranjan Gogoi. The motion for his election was moved by J. P. Nadda, Leader of the House in the Rajya Sabha. His unanimous election reflects consensus and cooperation in Indian parliamentary democracy and confidence across party lines.

questions.question: The ‘Vishwa Sutra’ initiative is associated with which sector?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Textiles launched the ‘Vishwa Sutra – Weaves of India for the World’ initiative to globalise Indian handlooms. It was showcased at the 61st Femina Miss India held in Bhubaneswar, Odisha. The initiative is developed by the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handlooms) in collaboration with the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT). It brings together 30 handloom weaves from different Indian states, each reinterpreted with design inspirations from 30 countries. The objective is to present Indian textiles in a contemporary global design framework and enhance international appeal.

questions.question: Col. Sher Jung National Park is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Himachal Pradesh High Court quashed a notification declaring Eco-Sensitive Zone (ESZ) around the Col. Sher Jung National Park, also known as Simbalbara National Park, is located in the Paonta Valley, Himachal Pradesh. It is named after freedom fighter and environmentalist Colonel Sher Jung. The park lies in the lower to upper Shiwalik ranges with elevations between 350–700 metres. It is situated at the confluence of the Himalayas, Gangetic plains, and semi-arid biogeographic regions. It shares ecological connectivity with Kalesar National Park.

questions.question: India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park is being established in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park is being established in Ladakh on the banks of the Indus River to preserve ancient rock art. Petroglyphs are prehistoric carvings or engravings on rock surfaces depicting symbols, figures, and inscriptions. The park aims to protect endangered petroglyphs threatened by tourism, infrastructure development, and environmental factors. It will relocate and conserve carvings from vulnerable and isolated sites across Ladakh for safe preservation and public display.

questions.question: Designer rice has been developed by which institute?

options.label: A
options.text: ICAR-Indian Agricultural Research Institute, New Delhi

options.label: B
options.text: CSIR–National Institute for Interdisciplinary Science and Technology

answer.label: B
answer.text: CSIR–National Institute for Interdisciplinary Science and Technology

questions.explanation: Scientists at CSIR National Institute for Interdisciplinary Science and Technology have developed “Designer Rice,” a functional food to address malnutrition. It is created by processing broken rice into flour, enriching it with protein, fibre, and micronutrients like iron, folic acid, and vitamin B12, and reforming it into rice-like grains. The technology has been transferred to Tata Consumer Products Limited for large-scale production. Unlike regular rice, it has higher protein (over 20%) and a low glycemic index (below 55). It helps manage blood sugar levels, making it beneficial for diabetics.

questions.question: The Market Intervention Scheme (MIS) is a component of which initiative?
questions.explanation: The Market Intervention Scheme is a component of Pradhan Mantri Annadata Aay Sanrakshan Abhiyan (PM-AASHA), under which the government recently approved procurement of 20 lakh metric tonnes of potatoes. The scheme is implemented at the request of State or Union Territory governments to procure perishable crops like tomato, onion, and potato that do not have a Minimum Support Price. Its objective is to protect farmers from distress sales during bumper production when market prices fall below the cost of production. It is implemented by the Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.

questions.question: A new plant species Stauranthera aureoglossa has been discovered in which state?
questions.explanation: A new plant species Stauranthera aureoglossa has been discovered in Arunachal Pradesh. It grows in mid-elevation evergreen forests of Kamle and Upper Subansiri districts. The plant is a perennial herb found on damp rocks near streams and rivulets. It has a bell-shaped, bluish-purple flower with no basal spur (tubular projection). A unique yellow, tongue-like patch is present inside the flower. The term “aureoglossa” means golden tongue, describing this feature. Leaves are diamond or spear-shaped.

questions.question: Aspero, that was recently seen in news, is a Late Preceramic site located in which country?
questions.explanation: Archaeologists discovered a sky-observation structure at Áspero, revealing early scientific practices. Áspero is a Late Preceramic site of the Norte Chico (Caral-Supe) civilization, one of the oldest in the Americas. It is located in Peru near the Supe River, close to the Pacific Ocean. It flourished during the Late Archaic period (before 3000 Before Christ (BC) to around 1800 BC). It was a major fishing, trade, and urban centre linked to Caral. The site has platform mounds and sunken plazas showing advanced planning.

questions.question: Operation Gangstran Te Vaar was launched by which state government?
questions.explanation: Punjab Police launched Operation Gangstran Te Vaar to curb gangster activities. The operation has led to a decline of up to 69% in gangster-related murders and firing incidents. A 100% reduction in gangster-linked murder cases has been reported since launch. Public participation has increased through anonymous reporting mechanisms. A toll-free Anti-Gangster Helpline (93946-93946) receives about 175 calls per month. Citizens report extortion calls and criminal activities via the helpline.
